name: Product Conversion
summary: Converts an existing position from one product type to another (e.g. intraday to delivery / carry-forward, or vice versa) via a POST call. Returns a simple Ok / Not_Ok status indicating conversion success or failure.

request_params.name: jData*
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list.

json_fields.name: tsym
json_fields.description: Unique id of contract on which order was placed. Can't be modified, must be the same as that of original order. (use url encoding to avoid special char error for symbols like M&M)

json_fields.name: qty
json_fields.description: Quantity to be converted [If qty is junk value other than numbers].

json_fields.name: uid
json_fields.description: User id of the logged in user.

json_fields.name: prd
json_fields.description: Product to which the user wants to convert position.

json_fields.name: trantype
json_fields.description: B -> BUY, S -> SELL [transtype should be 'B' or 'S' else reject].

json_fields.name: postype
json_fields.description: Converting Day or Carry forward position

response_fields.name: stat
response_fields.description: Position conversion success or failure indication.

response_fields.name: emsg
response_fields.description: This will be present only if Position conversion fails.

notes: Section spans PDF pages 29-30; ends where "PLACE GTT ORDER" begins. The documented jData-field table lists exch, tsym, qty, uid, actid, prd, prevprd, trantype, postype, ordersource — it does NOT list prc or prctyp, even though both appear in the curl example; those two extra example fields are not part of the field spec. The prd and prevprd "Possible value" cells are blank in the doc (free-text product codes such as H/C/I are implied by the example but not enumerated). Only ordersource is optional (no trailing *); all other jData fields are required.
issues: The documented jData-field table (exch, tsym, qty, uid, actid, prd, prevprd, trantype, postype, ordersource) does not list "prc"/"prctyp" even though they appear in the curl example, so those two extra example fields are recorded only in notes, not as json_fields. prd and prevprd possible-value cells are blank in the doc (free-text product codes such as H/C/I implied by the example but not enumerated in the table).
